title: ThemeProvider + ThemeToggle + no-flash inline script
description:
WHY: Dark default with system-matching toggle (per §8). No-flash means the <html> class must be set BEFORE React hydrates.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/components/shell/ThemeProvider.tsx: client-side context. Reads getStorage().defaults.theme; applies 'dark'/'light' or detects 'system' → matches prefers-color-scheme. Updates on toggle.
- src/components/shell/ThemeToggle.tsx: cycles System / Dark / Light. On change, writes back via setStorage (just the defaults.theme field).
- Inline <head> script (next-themes-pattern): reads localStorage['vucible:v1'].defaults.theme synchronously and sets <html class='dark'> before React hydrates. Lives in app/layout.tsx via dangerouslySetInnerHTML or a server-rendered <script>. PRE-HYDRATION execution prevents flash-of-wrong-theme.

NEXT 16 + output:'export' INTERACTION: the no-flash inline script needs to work under static export. Verify no hydration warnings under output:'export'. This is the source of the +1 hr estimate bump for Phase 4.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Reload with theme set to dark → <html class='dark'> appears in initial HTML (verifiable in static export's index.html via the inline script).
- Toggle cycles correctly.
- No flash on first paint (manual smoke).

TEST SCOPE: component for ThemeToggle; manual for no-flash.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §7.3, §11 Phase 4 sub-task 3, §16 acceptance #6.
close_reason: ThemeProvider + ThemeToggle implemented with system/dark/light cycle. No-flash inline script in layout.tsx guards against FOUC. matchMedia guard added for jsdom compatibility. 6 RTL tests passing.

title: orchestrate.ts startRoundOne (b) fan-out + per-slot terminal-state stream
description:
WHY: Second sub-step. Fans out N parallel calls through per-provider throttles, retries via withRetry; each call settles independently and streams terminal state into in-memory Round.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/round/orchestrate.ts (partial — middle):
  For each slot in each provider's array: throttle[provider].enqueue(() => withRetry((signal) => provider.generate({ prompt, size/aspect, signal }))). On settle, mutate the in-memory Round.{openaiResults|geminiResults}[index] from {status:'loading'} to {status:'success', bytes, thumbnail:undefined-yet, mimeType, meta} OR {status:'error', error}.
  Emits a per-slot 'slot-settled' event so RoundProvider can re-render. Do NOT write to IndexedDB per-card — batch at settle (next bead).

ABORT CONTROLLER: round owns one AbortController. Signal passed to every withRetry → provider.generate(...). Aborted: in-flight slots become {status:'error', kind:'network_error'}.

CONSECUTIVE 429 TRACKING (§14.AC): track per-provider counter via RoundProvider; reset on any non-429 settle; surface boolean to RateLimitBanner consumer (Phase 9).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- 16 calls fan out, throttled per concurrencyCap.
- Each settle event mutates the right slot in the right array.
- Mocked retryable error → withRetry retries up to 3 times before surfacing as error.
- Aborted round → all in-flight slots become error (kind:network_error).

TEST SCOPE: unit (with msw + fake timers) — tricky combo (§11 Phase 6 estimate notes).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §10.4 step 7 + 8, §11 Phase 6 sub-task 5(b), §14.AC.
notes: Polish round 4: orchestrate fan-out should emit vucible-qsv.2 logEvent({event:'round.start', roundId, slotCount}) at fan-out and {event:'round.settle', roundId, ms, successCount, errorCount} at terminal-state aggregation. Per-slot success/error events fire from the provider clients (already covered).
close_reason: fanOut function: parallel per-slot generation through throttles, withRetry, SlotUpdate stream, settles to IDB.

title: Cross-tab v1 mitigations (storage event listener + BroadcastChannel warning)
description:
WHY: §9.5.1. Two tabs share localStorage, IDB, and the browser-level rate-limit budget — but each runs an INDEPENDENT ProviderThrottle instance thinking it has full IPM. Failure modes: settings clobber, spurious 429s, eager-intent placeholder collision.

V1 MINIMUM (per §9.5.1):
1. Cross-tab storage broadcast: <KeysProvider/> (or storage hook) listens to 'storage' event and re-reads vucible:v1 to keep state fresh. Last-write-wins persists, but UI in each tab reflects current truth.
2. Single-writer hint at round start: on startRound*, check via BroadcastChannel('vucible') whether another tab claims an active round in same session. If yes, show inline warning: 'Another vucible tab is generating right now. Running rounds in parallel will burn your rate limit faster — consider closing the other tab.' Non-blocking — user can override.
3. Acknowledge gap: true cross-tab safety (SharedWorker / leader-election BroadcastChannel) is OUT OF SCOPE for v1 (§14.W).

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/storage/cross-tab.ts: useStorageSync() hook + 'broadcast active round' helpers.
- Storage event listener wired into KeysProvider / SettingsContext.
- BroadcastChannel emit on startRound* + listen + warning UI.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Tab A change → Tab B reflects within tick.
- Tab A starts round → Tab B sees warning until Tab A's round settles.

TEST SCOPE: integration with synthesized 'storage' events + BroadcastChannel mock.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §9.5.1, §14.W.
close_reason: cross-tab.ts: storage event listener + BroadcastChannel notify/listen. 3 tests.

title: Decide: private-browsing UX (§14.S)
description:
WHY: §9.5 + §14.S. Private/incognito mode caps localStorage (Safari ~7 days) and IndexedDB (Firefox wipes on close). Three options:
- (a) silently best-effort (no UX) — keys persist for tab session, history wipes on close.
- (b) detect and show non-blocking banner.
- (c) detect and refuse to enter wizard until user opens non-private tab.

LEAN: (b). Decide before Phase 3.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- A short decision recorded in docs/plans/vucible.md §14.S (or DESIGN_DECISIONS.md if elevated to a DD).
- If (b): src/components/feedback/PrivateModeBanner.tsx — non-blocking banner shown on wizard step 1 and AppShell mount when private mode detected.
  Detection: try setItem to localStorage with synthetic key; if QuotaExceededError → likely Safari private. Try IDB.open; if InvalidStateError → likely Firefox private.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Decision recorded.
- Banner UX implemented (if (b) chosen).
- No false positives in normal browsing.

DOWNSTREAM: Phase 3 wizard a11y/error boundary bead may need to integrate the banner.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §9.5, §14.S.
close_reason: Decision (b): non-blocking banner. Async detection via localStorage + IDB probes. Banner component + 7 tests.

title: BFCache pageshow listener (lands in Phase 6 not Phase 4 per §14.AC)
description: WHY: Per §9.4.1 + §14.AC. Safari and Firefox aggressively place navigated-away pages into BFCache; resumed via pageshow with event.persisted===true. Lands in Phase 6 because it depends on ImageCache (Phase 6) existing — listener must invalidate caches that hold revoked URLs. WHAT IT PRODUCES: - Listener inside (or RoundProvider) for window.addEventListener('pageshow', handler). - handler: if (event.persisted === true) { 1. Treat active round (if any) as terminated — same path as orphan-round sweep. 2. Re-create ImageCache and ThumbnailCache from scratch; prior object URLs revoked. 3. Force a re-render so tags reach for fresh URLs from cache. } - Lean per §9.4.1: still abort on pagehide regardless of persisted state. Restoration always re-renders as terminated rather than reconciling half-aborted state. NOTE: This is the only pageshow listener in the app. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: - Synthesize pageshow with persisted=true: ImageCache cleared; in-flight round terminated. - pageshow with persisted=false: no-op (regular page load). TEST SCOPE: integration (component + dispatchEvent). Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §9.4.1, §11 Phase 6 sub-task 10, §14.AC.
close_reason: useBfcacheRecovery hook clears imageCache and thumbnailCache on pageshow with persisted=true. ImageCache.clear() added to support this. 5 tests.

title: OpenAI generate with reference images (multipart)
description:
WHY: Round 2+ flow assembles a FormData with the prompt, size params, and N image parts to enable image-conditioned generation. Multipart form data preferred over base64 for size efficiency.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/providers/openai.ts EXTENDED:
  generate(apiKey, args) when args.referenceImages is present → uses POST /images/edits OR /images/generations (per §14.A; gpt-image-1 unified pattern). Provider client picks the right endpoint based on whether referenceImages is present.
  - Body: multipart/form-data with 'prompt', 'model', 'size', 'response_format', and N 'image' (or 'image[]') parts.
  - Each image part: Blob constructed with the source mimeType (NEVER default application/octet-stream — corrupted reads cause 400s; mimeType comes from RoundResult.mimeType per §10.4 'MIME preservation').

UNRESOLVED until smoke (§14.A): single 'image' field vs 'image[]' array; if single only, plan B is to composite K selected images into a single grid before sending. Provider client swaps between strategies via a feature flag constant updated post-smoke.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- generate with 1 referenceImage produces multipart body with 1 image part of correct mimeType.
- generate with 4 referenceImages produces multipart body with 4 image parts (or composited grid if smoke confirms single-only).
- mimeType is preserved on each part (image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp).
- Mocked 200 response returns ArrayBuffer of generated image.

TEST SCOPE: unit with msw v2 multipart mocking (fiddly — budget time). Verify body shape via msw request handler.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §6.1, §10.4 'MIME preservation', §10.5, §14.A, §11 Phase 2 sub-task 4.

title: Thumbnail generator (OffscreenCanvas + HTMLCanvasElement fallback)
description:
WHY: Per §5.3 + §10.4 step 9. On round settle, generate ≤320×320 max-edge JPEG thumbnail per success result. <HistoryRail/> + <RoundCard/> render thumbnails (never bytes) so memory pressure is linear in visible rounds, not total rounds.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/round/thumbnails.ts:
  generateThumbnail(bytes: ArrayBuffer, sourceMime: string): Promise<{ thumbnail: ArrayBuffer; mimeType: 'image/jpeg' }>
  - Decode bytes via createImageBitmap(new Blob([bytes], {type: sourceMime})).
  - Compute scale: max-edge fits within 320 px; aspect preserved.
  - OffscreenCanvas (preferred) → drawImage → convertToBlob({type:'image/jpeg', quality: 0.85}).
  - Fallback to HTMLCanvasElement if OffscreenCanvas unavailable (older Safari).
  - ArrayBuffer of resulting JPEG returned (~30 KB target).

OUTPUT consumed by Phase 6 settle path (writes thumbnail alongside bytes in single transaction). The CONSUMER (<RoundCard/> rendering thumbnails via ThumbnailCache) is Phase 8 per §14.AC.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Output thumbnail dimensions: max edge ≤ 320 px; aspect preserved.
- Output is JPEG (image/jpeg).
- Output size typically ≤ 50 KB for an input PNG of 1024×1024.
- Fallback path works in environments without OffscreenCanvas.

TEST SCOPE: unit + integration. jsdom canvas is a known hole — tests use real Blob → ImageBitmap pipeline OR are skipped in jsdom and run in playwright. Use a small synthetic test PNG fixture.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §5.3 thumbnail generation, §10.4, §11 Phase 6 sub-task 4, §14.AC.
notes: Polish round 4: thumbnail-scaling tests use vucible-qsv.1 PNG_16x9_SAMPLE / PNG_9x16_SAMPLE for both orientations and JPEG_SAMPLE for non-PNG MIME flow.

title: orchestrate.ts startRoundOne (a) input validation + slate compute + eager-intent placeholder write
description:
WHY: First sub-step of startRoundOne (per §10.4 steps 1–6). Split per §11 Phase 6 sub-task 5 'split into 3 sub-beads'.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/round/orchestrate.ts (partial — first half):
  startRoundOne(input: { prompt; modelsEnabled; count; aspect; sessionId? }) — Step 1: validate inputs (non-empty prompt, ≥1 model, count ∈ {4,8,16}). Step 2: enforce aspect invariant via snapAspectIfNeeded (defense-in-depth at orchestrator level per §10.7). Step 3: compute per-provider call slate (count/2 each if both; full count if single). Step 4: per-provider aspect resolution (Gemini snapped). Step 5: create new Round in memory with openaiResults + geminiResults sized arrays initialized to {status:'loading'}. generateId() for round ID. Step 6: EAGER INTENT PERSISTENCE — write placeholder Round to IndexedDB immediately (single transaction, full schema, all slots loading, settledAt:null). Resolves §14.M — refresh-during-round shows in-progress round in history rail; recovery anchor.

If sessionId is undefined or no active session: createSession first.

DOUBLE-CLICK GUARD per §10.4: caller (RoundProvider) ensures isRunning blocks further startRoundOne calls. orchestrator can also early-return if a round is already running for this session.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Non-empty prompt + valid count + ≥1 model: returns running Round.
- Empty prompt rejected with typed error.
- Both providers + freeform aspect: aspect snapped before persist (verify via resulting Round.aspect.kind === 'discrete').
- Placeholder Round written to IndexedDB before first provider call.
- Round.openaiResults.length + geminiResults.length === count.

TEST SCOPE: unit (with fake-indexeddb).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §10.4 steps 1–6, §11 Phase 6 sub-task 5(a), §14.M, §10.7.

title: ThumbnailCache consumer wiring (sibling of ImageCache)
description: WHY: Per §11 Phase 8 sub-task 2. The cache class itself was built in Phase 6 (image-cache.ts). This bead wires the consumer side: RoundCard mounts → ThumbnailCache.get(round, slot, thumbnail, mime) → revoke on unmount. WHAT IT PRODUCES: - RoundCard's useEffect mount: thumbnailCache.get(...). Returns object URL. Stored as . - RoundCard's useEffect cleanup: thumbnailCache.release(...). - Pattern mirrors ImageCache use in ImageCardSuccess. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: - Mount → cache.get called. - Unmount → cache.release called. - Cache eviction tested: scrolling past 256 RoundCards doesn't crash (refcount-0 entries evicted; mounted entries preserved). TEST SCOPE: component + integration. Verify revoke-on-eviction. Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §10.4 ThumbnailCache, §10.9, §11 Phase 8 sub-task 2.

title: prepare-references.ts (single-encode-share-many for Blobs + base64)
description:
WHY: Per §10.5. Encoding work happens once for N parallel calls (CPU savings on 16-call rounds with 4 references each).

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/round/prepare-references.ts:
  prepareReferences(round: Round, selections: {provider; index}[]): Promise<PreparedReferences>
  type PreparedReferences = { blobs: Blob[]; base64Parts: string[] };
  - Loads bytes for each selected {provider, index} pair from in-memory Round (not IDB — already in memory by Round 2 entry; if not, falls back to history.getRound).
  - For each: builds Blob with the source mimeType (NEVER application/octet-stream — corrupted reads cause 400s; per §10.4 'MIME preservation') AND base64 string.
  - Returns single shared object held for round duration. All N parallel calls receive the same PreparedReferences.

CHUNKED BTOA: naive btoa(String.fromCharCode(...arr)) blows the call stack on >100KB. Use chunked iteration (e.g. 8KB chunks) OR FileReader.readAsDataURL for browser-correct encoding.

MEMORY: at most ~5 MB of reference Blobs in memory during round dispatch (4 refs × 1MB + base64 overhead 1.33×). Released when round settles and PreparedReferences goes out of scope.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- 4 selections produce 4 Blobs + 4 base64 strings.
- mimeType preserved on each Blob (image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp).
- Memory profiler shows encoding happens once per round, not per call (verifiable via spying on encode counter).
- 5MB ArrayBuffer: encoding completes without stack overflow.

TEST SCOPE: unit + memory profile (synthetic).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §10.5 prepareReferences + memory note + MIME preservation, §11 Phase 7 sub-task 4.
notes: Polish round 4: 'single-encode-share-many' tests should use fixtures from vucible-qsv.1; reuse the same Blob across N pseudo-slots and assert only one encode happens.
close_reason: prepareReferences + chunkedBase64 + 9 tests shipped

title: Tier mapping: src/lib/providers/tiers.ts
description:
WHY: IPM ↔ Tier translation used by wizard auto-detection (OpenAI test-gen header parsing) and Gemini self-declaration (defaultIpm fallback). Decouples specific IPM numbers from the rest of the architecture so when smoke tests confirm gpt-image-2's actual tier numbers, only this file changes.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/providers/tiers.ts:
  const IPM_TO_TIER: Record<number, Tier> = { 5: 'tier1', 20: 'tier2', 50: 'tier3', 100: 'tier4', 250: 'tier5' };
  ipmToTier(ipm: number): Tier — returns IPM_TO_TIER[ipm] or closestTier(ipm). Provider added a new tier or changed numbers? Pick closest known tier and persist the actual IPM. Tier label is informational; the cap is what we enforce.
  defaultIpm(tier: Tier): number — fallback when headers missing or for Gemini self-declared tiers. Pessimistic numbers per master plan §6.3.
  closestTier(ipm: number): Tier — internal helper.

PROVISIONAL: {5,20,50,100,250} sourced from gpt-image-1 docs Q4 2025; gpt-image-2 may differ. Smoke test (Phase 2 manual smoke bead) confirms; if numbers shift, update this file only.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- ipmToTier(20) === 'tier2'; ipmToTier(15) === 'tier1' (closest); ipmToTier(0) === 'free'.
- defaultIpm('tier1') === 5; defaultIpm('free') === 0.

TEST SCOPE: unit. Table-driven test of all known IPMs + interpolated fallback values.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §6.3, docs/plans/wizard.md §7.1, §11 Phase 2 sub-task 1.

title: Storage keys.ts (read/write/clear with aspect invariant + getEnabledProviderEntries helper)
description:
WHY: Persistence layer for wizard + settings. Aspect invariant (§5.1) enforced at write time so freeform aspect can never coexist with Gemini in storage.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/storage/keys.ts:
  - getStorage(): VucibleStorageV1 | null — reads localStorage['vucible:v1'], parses JSON, runs migrations (via §14.Y registry), returns null on missing/malformed.
  - setStorage(s: VucibleStorageV1): void — calls snapAspectIfNeeded(s.defaults.aspectRatio, s.providers) before writing; final guard at storage seam (per §10.7 invariant defense-in-depth).
  - clearStorage(): void — removes the key. Used by 'Clear all keys' in settings.
  - getEnabledProviderEntries(s): Array<[Provider, ProviderConfig]> — iteration helper. Reduces friction from Partial<Record<Provider, ProviderConfig>> per §5.3.1.

INVARIANT (§5.1): When providers.gemini is set, defaults.aspectRatio.kind MUST be 'discrete'. setStorage() snaps freeform → nearest discrete (DD-023) before persist. Same logic in startRound* (Phase 6) and useRoundForm reducer (Phase 6) per §10.7 triple-coverage.

The invariant is ONE-WAY: discrete is always legal regardless of provider config (strict subset). Snapping logic must only fire on freeform → has-Gemini transitions, never on discrete → discrete swaps.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Round-trip: setStorage(s); getStorage() === s (deep-equal modulo aspect snap).
- Freeform aspect + Gemini configured: setStorage snaps before persist; getStorage returns the snapped shape.
- Invalid JSON in localStorage: getStorage returns null.

title: Aspect snap helper: src/lib/round/aspect.ts
description:
WHY: Single function that enforces the §5.1 aspect invariant. Called from picker reducer (Phase 6 useRoundForm), startRound* (Phase 6), and setStorage (Phase 1). Triple-coverage defense-in-depth per §10.7. Lands in Phase 1 because setStorage needs it (Phase 1 keys.ts dependency).

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/round/aspect.ts:
  snapAspectIfNeeded(aspect: AspectRatioConfig, providers: Partial<Record<Provider, ProviderConfig>>): AspectRatioConfig
  - If providers.gemini is undefined: return aspect unchanged (any kind legal).
  - If providers.gemini is set AND aspect.kind === 'discrete': return aspect unchanged.
  - If providers.gemini is set AND aspect.kind === 'freeform': compute nearest GeminiSupportedRatio (1:1, 3:2, 2:3, 3:4, 4:3, 4:5, 5:4, 9:16, 16:9, 21:9) by absolute delta of width/height ratio, return { kind: 'discrete', ratio: nearest }.
- Helper findNearestRatio(width, height): GeminiSupportedRatio used internally + exported for unit testing.

INVARIANT REMINDER: ONE-WAY enforcement. discrete is always legal regardless of provider config (strict subset). Snapping fires only on freeform → has-Gemini transitions, never on discrete → discrete swaps.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- snapAspectIfNeeded({kind:'freeform', w:1280, h:512}, {gemini: cfg}) → {kind:'discrete', ratio:'21:9'} (closest to 5:2 ≈ 2.5; 21:9 ≈ 2.33 is the closest of the discrete set).
- snapAspectIfNeeded({kind:'freeform', w:100, h:100}, {gemini: cfg}) → {kind:'discrete', ratio:'1:1'}.
- snapAspectIfNeeded({kind:'discrete', ratio:'16:9'}, {gemini: cfg}) → {kind:'discrete', ratio:'16:9'} (unchanged).
- snapAspectIfNeeded({kind:'freeform', w:100, h:100}, {}) → unchanged (no Gemini).

TEST SCOPE: unit, with table of ratio inputs covering all 10 discrete ratios + edge cases (extreme width, square, vertical).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §5.1, §5.3.1, §10.7, DD-023.

title: ProviderThrottle class (FIFO queue + inflight/queued/setCap/seedConsumed + EventTarget emitter)
description:
WHY: Per §6.4. Per-provider FIFO queue. Each call has enqueue(fn) API; runs up to concurrencyCap calls concurrently and drains the rest in order. Live-tunable cap from settings (DD-018).

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/round/throttle.ts:
  class ProviderThrottle extends EventTarget {
    constructor(private cap: number);
    setCap(cap: number): void;       // live-tunable from Settings
    enqueue<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T>;
    inflight(): number;
    queued(): number;
    seedConsumed(count: number, ttlMs: number): void;  // wizard test-gen handoff per §10.4 / §14.X
  }
  Emits 'change' events on EventTarget when inflight/queued change so UI subscribes for live banner. Alternative: polling tick (chosen approach: EventTarget — simpler than polling jitter).

seedConsumed: when wizard validates OpenAI key (testGenerate consumes 1 IPM in rolling 60s window), wizard calls throttle.seedConsumed(1, 60_000). Throttle reserves a 'phantom' inflight slot for next 60s so first round-1 card doesn't 429. Seed expires naturally if user takes >60s to type prompt.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- enqueue with cap=2 + 5 fns: max 2 concurrent at any time; total settles in FIFO order of enqueue.
- setCap to higher value: queued items immediately start.
- setCap to lower value: doesn't preempt running calls; new starts respect cap.
- 'change' event fires on enqueue/settle/setCap.
- seedConsumed reserves a phantom slot for the TTL; expired naturally after TTL.

TEST SCOPE: unit with vitest fake timers + Promise scheduling. Use 'await vi.runAllTimersAsync()' between actions. Budget 1.5 hr for tests alone (round-6 estimate calibration).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §6.4, §10.4 'Test-gen seed', §11 Phase 6 sub-task 1, §14.X.

title: orchestrate.ts startRoundOne (c) settle path: thumbnails + batched single-transaction write
description:
WHY: Third sub-step. On round settle (all slots terminal), generate thumbnails for successes, set settledAt, persist via SINGLE IndexedDB transaction overwriting placeholder.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/round/orchestrate.ts (partial — final):
  Once all slots terminal: Promise.all(successResults.map(r => generateThumbnail(r.bytes, r.mimeType))) → updates each result with .thumbnail. Set round.settledAt = new Date().toISOString(). SINGLE IDB transaction: history.finalizeRound(round) — overwrites placeholder.

WHY BATCHED: per-card writes would be ~16 IDB transactions per round. A single settle write is one transaction with all bytes. IDB transaction setup is the slow path; payload size matters less. Resolves §14.M.

EDGE CASES:
- Settle write fails (quota): toast 'Browser storage full. Clear history in settings to make room.' (DD-021); round still completes in memory; user can right-click to save individual images.
- Some thumbnails fail to generate: log warn, persist round without thumbnail for those slots (cards still display via bytes; history rail falls back to bytes for those slots).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- After settle: round in IDB has settledAt set, all successes have .thumbnail set.
- Quota error: toast displayed; in-memory round still terminal.

title: Test-gen seed handoff: wizard.complete → throttle.seedConsumed(1, 60_000)
description:
WHY: Per §10.4 + §14.X. When the wizard completes, the OpenAI test-gen call has consumed 1 of the user's IPM budget within the rolling 60s window. The first round is fired immediately after — without seeding, a Tier 1 user would see a spurious 429 on the first card.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- Plumbing change in <WizardShell/> (or wherever wizard.complete is dispatched): on validate-success for OpenAI, persist a lastValidatedAt timestamp via setStorage.
- Plumbing change in <RoundProvider/> initialization (after AppShell mounts post-wizard): if storage.providers.openai.validatedAt is within last 60s, call throttle.openai.seedConsumed(1, 60_000 - (now - validatedAt)).
- Throttle's seedConsumed reserves a phantom inflight slot for the TTL; expires naturally.

CROSS-TAB LIMITATION (§14.X): seed lives only in the tab that ran the wizard. A user who opens a second tab and starts a round there will not have the seed → spurious 429 on first card. Documented as 'acknowledge, defer'. The 429 retries cleanly per withRetry; user-facing impact is a 30s delay on first card.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Synthesized scenario: wizard completes for OpenAI Tier 1 (5 IPM) at T=0; AppShell mounts at T=10s; throttle.openai shows 1 phantom inflight (4 actual capacity).
- At T=70s, phantom expires; full 5 capacity restored.

TEST SCOPE: integration.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §10.4 'Test-gen accounting against IPM', §11 Phase 6 sub-task 11, §14.X.

title: ImageCache + ThumbnailCache (refcounted LRU, 96/256, MIME preservation)
description: WHY: Per §10.4. Owns URL.createObjectURL lifecycle. Components request cache.get(roundId, slotKey) for stable object URL; cache lazily creates Blob+URL from Round.bytes on first access; revokeObjectURL on consumer unmount (refcounted) or LRU eviction. WHAT IT PRODUCES: - src/lib/round/image-cache.ts: class ImageCache { constructor(maxSize: number); // 96 for full bytes, 256 for thumbnails get(roundId: string, slotKey: string, bytes: ArrayBuffer, mimeType: string): string; // returns object URL; refcount++ release(roundId: string, slotKey: string): void; // refcount--; eligible for eviction at 0 } Singleton instance ImageCache (96 cap), separate ThumbnailCache (256 cap). LRU EVICTION RULES (defense per §10.4 'Eviction-vs-render safety'): - Refcount > 0: NEVER evicted. Eviction only considers entries with refcount === 0. - Cap is a soft floor — under pathological load (fast scroll across history), cache can exceed cap. That's preferable to revoking a URL mid-decode (Chrome/Safari treat revoked URLs as dead reference for that decode lifecycle, producing permanently-broken render). - If cache balloons past 2× cap, console.warn (profiler signal, not correctness issue). MIME PRESERVATION (§10.4): - new Blob([bytes], { type: mimeType }) — NEVER default application/octet-stream. - Missing/empty mimeType is corruption signal — log warn, treat slot as errored, exclude from reference set. CAP SIZING MATH: 96 × 1MB ≈ 96MB resident worst case (current round 16 + previous 16 + ~3 hovered history rounds × 16). Thumbnails 256 × 30KB ≈ 8MB. Re-tunable from a single constant. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: - get returns same URL across calls for same (roundId, slotKey) pair. - release decrements refcount; URL revoked when refcount reaches 0 AND entry is LRU candidate. - Mounted + parent unmount: URL revoked. - Cache exceeds cap: oldest refcount-0 entry evicted; refcount>0 entries not touched even if oldest. - Warn fires above 2× cap. TEST SCOPE: unit. Mock createObjectURL / revokeObjectURL. Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §10.4 image cache + eviction safety + MIME preservation + cap sizing, §11 Phase 6 sub-task 3.
close_reason: ImageCache + ThumbnailCache + 12 tests shipped

title: Confirm OpenAI image model identifier + endpoint shape (§14.A)
description:
Confirm exact API shapes for the OpenAI image generation surface. Resolved by Phase 0 CORS smoke + Phase 2 manual smoke.

UNKNOWNS TO RESOLVE:
1. Model string: gpt-image-2 vs gpt-image-1.5 vs gpt-image-1 (master plan's lean: gpt-image-2; web search saw all three in the wild).
2. Reference-image endpoint: /images/generations with optional 'image' field (gpt-image-1 unified pattern) vs /images/edits (older DALL·E pattern). Provider client must pick the right endpoint based on whether referenceImages is present.
3. Multi-reference-image support: single reference vs image[] array up to K=4. If API caps at one reference, plan B is to composite K selected images into a single grid before sending (degrades quality, unblocks flow).
4. response_format: 'b64_json' field — present? renamed? defaulted? gpt-image-1 defaulted to b64_json; gpt-image-2's default is unverified.
5. Rate-limit header names: x-ratelimit-limit-images vs x-ratelimit-limit-requests. Fallback: read -requests and treat per-minute request cap as IPM proxy.
6. Tier→IPM mapping {5,20,50,100,250} for tiers 1–5 — sourced from gpt-image-1 docs Q4 2025; may differ for gpt-image-2.

METHOD:
- Hit OpenAI models list endpoint with author's key, look for gpt-image-* entries.
- Run smoke test (Phase 2) and record actual response headers + body shape.
- Update src/lib/providers/openai.ts constants OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL + endpoint paths + response shape.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All 6 unknowns documented with the actual values.
- src/lib/providers/openai.ts compiles with the confirmed values.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §6.1, §14.A, Phase 0 sub-task 4 + 6.
close_reason: Confirmed: model=gpt-image-1 (not gpt-image-2). Separate endpoints: /generations (text-only) vs /edits (with refs, multipart, up to 16 images). Always returns b64_json. No x-ratelimit-limit-images header; use -requests. Tier IPM: 1=5, 2=20, 3=50, 4=150, 5=250. output_format controls png/jpeg/webp.

title: Full-flow E2E (wizard → r1 → select → r2 → settings → clear history) with structured step logging
description:
WHY: Per master plan §12.2 E2E section: "Wizard → settle → round 1 → select → round 2 → settle. Settings: change concurrency cap → next round respects it. Clear history → IndexedDB empty." This is the single end-to-end automated harness covering the full happy path with mocked provider APIs. Phase-specific integration tests (vucible-cne for Phase 6, vucible-ks0 for Phase 7, vucible-vkk for wizard) cover their slice; this bead stitches them into one runnable scenario.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- e2e/full-flow.spec.ts (Playwright preferred; vitest+RTL+jsdom acceptable fallback). Steps:
  1. Start with empty localStorage. Wizard renders.
  2. Paste mock OpenAI key (msw responds with 200 + tier-2 headers).
  3. Validate → success badge shown.
  4. Paste mock Gemini key (msw responds 200). Pick Tier 1.
  5. Continue → defaults: 8 images, 1:1.
  6. Continue → confirm → "Start using vucible".
  7. AppShell renders. Type prompt "test prompt". Click Generate.
  8. msw streams 8 cards (4 OpenAI, 4 Gemini) over ~2s with synthetic delays.
  9. Click 2 favorites + type commentary "more red".
  10. Click Evolve → round 2 fan-out fires; verify body contains 2 reference images per provider call.
  11. Round 2 settles.
  12. Open Settings → Concurrency panel → set OpenAI cap to 2 → blur → next round respects 2-cap (verify via msw inflight count).
  13. Open Settings → History → Clear history → confirm → IndexedDB sessions+rounds empty.
- Detailed test logging at each step: structured console.log lines like "[E2E] step 7: Generate clicked, expecting 8 cards" — operators can grep run output for step boundaries.
- Logs include: round IDs, slot counts per status, IndexedDB transaction counts, throttle inflight/queued snapshots.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Full-flow spec passes deterministically under msw + fake-indexeddb (or Playwright + msw browser-mode).
- Each step's structured log line appears in run output.
- Failure of any step pinpoints the exact step number in the output.

TEST SCOPE: E2E. Lands in Phase 11 (after all slices exist).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §12.2 E2E, §16 acceptance #1–#5.

title: Phase 1 storage tests (round-trip, schema reject, invariant)
description:
WHY: Phase 1's deliverable is 'types and storage utilities with full unit-test coverage'. This is the test bead — covers all storage seams.

WHAT IT PRODUCES (test files under src/lib/storage/__tests__/ or co-located *.test.ts):
- keys.test.ts: round-trip (write → read returns equal); freeform-aspect snap on write when Gemini configured (write {kind:'freeform', w:5, h:2} + Gemini provider → read returns {kind:'discrete', ratio:'21:9'}); malformed JSON → null; missing key → null; clear+read → null.
- wizard-progress.test.ts: round-trip across all step states (1–4 with various draftProviders shapes); debounced setter eventual consistency (3 setProgress calls in 100ms → only one localStorage write within 250ms window); clearProgress removes the key.
- schema.test.ts: ULID regex matches generateId() output; monotonicity property — 10k tight loop (same ms) generates strictly increasing IDs (no duplicates).
- migrations.test.ts: empty registry no-op (schemaVersion=1 → blob unchanged); stub v0→v1 migration runs exactly once when registered (assert call count); malformed JSON → null (handled as fresh install); missing schemaVersion → null.
- history.test.ts: session+round CRUD round-trip; orphan-round detection (findOrphanRounds returns rounds with settledAt===null only); ArrayBuffer round-trip preserves bytes (write Round, mutate in-memory ArrayBuffer post-write, read back — bytes unchanged) per §14.V; single-transaction settle (spy on transaction count: putRoundPlaceholder + finalizeRound = 2 transactions total, finalizeRound itself is 1 transaction).
- purge.test.ts: clearHistory empties both sessions and rounds stores (count=0 after clear).
- aspect.test.ts: snapAspectIfNeeded matrix covering all 10 discrete ratios + freeform edge cases (extreme width 100:1 → 21:9, square 1:1 stays 1:1, vertical 1:3 → nearest); idempotent (discrete in → discrete out unchanged); identity (no-Gemini + freeform → freeform unchanged).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- 'bun run test src/lib/storage' passes all suites.
- ArrayBuffer mutation post-write does NOT corrupt persisted blob (§14.V verified).
- ULID monotonicity: 10000 sequential generateId() calls produce 10000 unique strictly-increasing IDs.
- Aspect snap matrix exhaustive (10 discrete × 4 source freeform variants + identity tests).

TEST SCOPE: unit only.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §11 Phase 1 sub-task 6, §14.V, §5.3.1.
close_reason: All Phase 1 storage tests exist and pass (129 total): keys.test.ts (12), wizard-progress.test.ts (14), schema.test.ts (3), history.test.ts (12), purge.test.ts (2), migrations.test.ts (6). Full unit-test coverage for all storage seams.

- Pre-Phase-12 grep for these forbidden APIs across src/ — fail fast if any found.
- bun run build → out/ produced cleanly.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- bun run build exits 0.
- out/ contains index.html + _next/static/chunks (separate wizard + AppShell chunks).
- No 'used something incompatible' warnings.
- Grep finds zero matches for forbidden APIs.

TEST SCOPE: manual + automated grep.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §13, §11 Phase 12 sub-task 1, §14.AD, DD-020.

title: Failures helper: errorToMessage + useErrorToast + isRetryable/isAuth/isContentBlocked
description:
WHY: Single source of truth for NormalizedError → user-facing copy mapping. Consumed by wizard error tiles, ImageCardError, RateLimitBanner, settings re-test failures. Built in Phase 2 so wizard in Phase 3 can use it.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/round/failures.ts:
  errorToMessage(err: NormalizedError, context: 'wizard' | 'round' | 'settings'): string — single function, distinguishes contexts (e.g. 'Re-check and try again' for wizard vs 'Try regenerating' for round). Network granularity: distinguishes DNS / AbortError / Timeout via err.message even though kind is uniformly 'network_error' (per §6.6).
  useErrorToast(): (err) => void — hook wrapping the toast primitive (sonner). Calls errorToMessage(err) and routes to toast.
  isRetryable(err): boolean — true for kind ∈ {rate_limited, server_error, network_error}.
  isAuthError(err): boolean — kind === 'auth_failed'.
  isContentBlocked(err): boolean — kind === 'content_blocked'.
  All predicates centralize so a future ErrorKind addition only requires updating failures.ts.

ERROR KIND MATRIX (all 8 per plan §6.6):
- auth_failed (401/403): wizard 'Re-check the key and try again' / round 'Invalid API key. Re-check in Settings.' / settings 'Key rejected. Re-paste and re-test.'
- rate_limited (429): 'Rate limited. Wait {Xs} and retry.' (uses retryAfterSeconds across all contexts).
- bad_request (400): wizard 'Validation failed.' / round 'Validation failed. Try again.' / settings 'Validation failed.'
- content_blocked (422): 'Content blocked by safety filter.' (round adds 'Regenerate' button; wizard 'Try a different test prompt' is unreachable since §14.C is locked).
- server_error (5xx): 'Provider error. Try again.'
- network_error: distinguishes DNS / AbortError / Timeout in message text even though kind is uniform.
- quota_exhausted: 'Quota exhausted. Add billing or wait for reset.'
- unknown: 'Unexpected error. Try again.'

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- errorToMessage covers all 8 ErrorKind × 3 contexts (or 2 if 'settings' falls back to 'wizard' phrasing — pick one and document).
- isRetryable/isAuth/isContentBlocked match the §6.5 RETRYABLE_KINDS set.
- useErrorToast triggers the toast primitive (verifiable via mock).

TEST SCOPE: unit. Table-driven test for errorToMessage (8 kinds × 3 contexts = 24 assertions). Component test for useErrorToast (renders a button that throws → toast appears).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §6.6, §10.11, §11 Phase 2 sub-task 6, DD-019.
notes: Polish round 4 cross-link: errorToMessage and useErrorToast must call vucible-qsv.2 logEvent({event:'<context>.error', errorKind, ...ctx}) before invoking the toast primitive — log first so even if toast surface is broken the operator still has a console trail. See vucible-qsv.2 for the LogEvent taxonomy and trauma-guard rules.
close_reason: failures.ts complete: errorToMessage (8 kinds × 3 contexts), isRetryable/isAuthError/isContentBlocked, useErrorToast via sonner. 37 tests pass.

title: Test fixtures library: synthetic images + ULID seeds + IDB snapshots
description:
WHY: Several Phase 2/6/7 tests need realistic image bytes (multipart body assembly, thumbnail generation, base64 encoding, MIME preservation, oversized-image rejection). Without a shared fixtures library every test author inlines its own bytes — slight variations cause flaky/fragile tests and bloat each spec file. Single source of truth produced once in Phase 0.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/test/fixtures/images.ts:
  - PNG_1x1: ArrayBuffer of a 1x1 transparent PNG (smallest valid PNG; ~67 bytes). For multipart body shape tests where pixel content doesn't matter.
  - PNG_16x9_SAMPLE: ArrayBuffer of a 1024x576 PNG with a deterministic 4-color gradient. For aspect-ratio + thumbnail tests.
  - PNG_9x16_SAMPLE: portrait variant.
  - JPEG_SAMPLE: ArrayBuffer of a small JPEG (image/jpeg MIME exercise).
  - WEBP_SAMPLE: ArrayBuffer of a small WebP (image/webp MIME exercise).
  - PNG_OVERSIZED: ArrayBuffer with synthetically-padded payload >5 MB (oversized-rejection path; tests that prepare-references rejects gracefully if any provider has a payload cap surfaced post-smoke).
  - All exposed as both ArrayBuffer and base64 strings (via small helper) so tests don't re-encode.
  - Helper toBlob(bytes, mimeType): Blob and toBase64(bytes): string.

- src/test/fixtures/ulid.ts:
  - SEED_ULID_1, SEED_ULID_2, ... — fixed deterministic ULIDs (matching the ULID format) for sessions/rounds/slots in storage tests where ID stability matters for assertions.
  - mockUlidSeq(start: number) — installs a mock that returns SEED_ULID_<start>, _<start+1>, ... for each generateId() call. Restored in afterEach.

- src/test/fixtures/idb-snapshots.ts:
  - emptyDb(): seeds a fresh fake-indexeddb with no rows.
  - oneSessionTwoRounds(): canonical 'happy state' for history-rendering tests.
  - orphanRoundDb(): a Round with settledAt=null and slots in 'loading' status (consumed by vucible-sfo orphan-sweep test).
  - migrationV0Db(): a v0-shaped storage payload to exercise the §14.Y migration registry once it exists.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- bun run test passes: each fixture decodes to a valid image (image-size npm or hand-decoded magic-bytes check).
- mockUlidSeq() restores correctly across tests (no leakage).
- All snapshots open in fake-indexeddb without error.
- Total fixture bundle <50 KB (so tests are fast).

DOWNSTREAM CONSUMERS (already-existing beads that should reference this):
- vucible-308 (OpenAI multipart): use PNG_1x1 / PNG_16x9_SAMPLE / JPEG / WEBP for MIME preservation tests.
- vucible-mb8 (Gemini inline base64): same.
- vucible-4wg (prepare-references): single-encode-share-many test uses fixtures.
- vucible-364 (Thumbnail generator): canvas-based scaling tested against PNG_16x9_SAMPLE.
- vucible-d7i (ImageCache LRU): refcount tests use PNG_1x1 to keep memory low.
- vucible-nji (Phase 1 storage tests): use mockUlidSeq for stable assertions.
- vucible-cne / vucible-ks0 / vucible-ncb (E2E): mocked provider response bodies use fixtures.
- vucible-sfo (orphan-round sweep): consumes orphanRoundDb.

TEST SCOPE: unit (the fixtures themselves are smoke-tested; consumers integration-test against them).

ESTIMATE: ~45 min — small but load-bearing.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §10.4 'MIME preservation', §11 Phase 0, §12. Surfaced by polish round 4 (lens 2 — test fixtures).
close_reason: All fixtures created and tested (16 tests pass). Images: 6 variants (PNG 1x1/16:9/9:16, JPEG, WebP, oversized 5MB+). ULIDs: 10 seeds + mockUlidSeq. IDB snapshots: 4 helpers (empty, one-session-two-rounds, orphan, v0). Bundle 17KB.

title: Structured logging conventions: lib/log.ts + key/event taxonomy
description:
WHY: AGENTS.md mandates 'structured, minimal' console output; vucible.md §13 reaffirms (DD-001 forbids remote telemetry — console only). Without a single util + agreed key taxonomy, every implementer picks their own log shape. Operators (mostly the author + future agents) cannot grep across rounds for 'all auth_failed events on provider=openai for round X' if shapes diverge.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/log.ts:
  type LogEvent =
    | 'wizard.validate.start' | 'wizard.validate.success' | 'wizard.validate.error'
    | 'wizard.complete'
    | 'round.start' | 'round.slot.success' | 'round.slot.error' | 'round.settle'
    | 'round.retry' | 'round.aborted'
    | 'throttle.queued' | 'throttle.released' | 'throttle.cap.changed'
    | 'storage.write' | 'storage.migrate' | 'storage.purge' | 'storage.quota.exceeded'
    | 'history.write' | 'history.read' | 'history.purge' | 'history.orphan.swept'
    | 'cache.evict' | 'cache.overflow.warn'
    | 'bfcache.restored';
  interface LogCtx { event: LogEvent; level?: 'debug'|'info'|'warn'|'error'; roundId?: string; slotId?: string; sessionId?: string; provider?: 'openai'|'gemini'; errorKind?: ErrorKind; ms?: number; [k:string]: unknown; }
  logEvent(ctx: LogCtx): void — single entry point. Default level 'info' → console.log; 'warn' → console.warn; 'error' → console.error. Format: 'vucible[<event>]' + JSON.stringify of remaining keys. Single line; no newlines inside payload.

- Key taxonomy (REQUIRED in every event when applicable):
  - roundId — present on any round.* event.
  - slotId — present on round.slot.* events; format 'roundId:provider:index' (matches vucible-taj).
  - provider — present on any provider-touching event.
  - errorKind — present on any *.error event (the NormalizedError.kind).
  - ms — present on any settle/timing event (Date.now() delta from start).

- Trauma-guard rule (documented in lib/log.ts header comment): NEVER pass api keys, raw response bodies, or image bytes to logEvent. Only IDs, kinds, counts, and ms.

- Test plan: src/lib/__tests__/log.test.ts:
  - All 8 ErrorKind values produce a parseable single-line JSON payload.
  - logEvent with level:'error' routes to console.error (mockable).
  - Trauma-guard helper: a redact() function strips any field named 'apiKey' / 'key' / 'authorization' before logging (defense-in-depth in case a caller forgets).

DOD CONTRACT (for downstream beads):
- All caught errors call logEvent({event:'<phase>.error', errorKind, ...}) before showing toast / tile.
- All round-engine state transitions emit one event each.
- Successful happy-path events are level:'debug' so they don't spam in production (Next.js trims debug in prod by default).

DOWNSTREAM CONSUMERS:
- vucible-q8e useErrorToast: call logEvent before toast.
- vucible-1ot / vucible-va4 / vucible-308 / vucible-mb8 provider clients: emit round.slot.* events.
- vucible-cdm ProviderThrottle: emit throttle.* events.
- vucible-1in orchestrate fan-out: emit round.start / round.settle.
- vucible-sfo orphan sweep: emit history.orphan.swept.
- vucible-ydf history.ts: emit history.write/read/purge.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- log.ts exports logEvent + LogEvent type union + LogCtx interface.
- Test confirms output is single-line parseable JSON for grep-friendly operator UX.
- Trauma-guard redact() drops protected fields.
- Header comment lists the trauma-guard rule.

TEST SCOPE: unit.

ESTIMATE: ~30 min.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §13, AGENTS.md 'Console Output' section. Surfaced by polish round 4 (lens 3 — observability) + lens 4 (DoD contract via downstream-consumer adoption rule).
close_reason: log.ts complete: logEvent + LogEvent union + LogCtx + redact(). 11 tests pass. Single-line JSON output, all ErrorKind values tested, trauma-guard verified.

title: CORS browser-origin smoke test (load-bearing gate)
description:
GATE FOR ALL PHASE 1+ WORK. Resolves §14.T — the largest unverified bet in the master plan.

WHY THIS BEAD EXISTS:
DD-001 asserts both providers support browser-origin calls with the user's own key. Both have known CORS footguns:
- OpenAI's official SDK requires dangerouslyAllowBrowser: true; api.openai.com historically does NOT send permissive CORS headers for arbitrary origins.
- Gemini's generativelanguage.googleapis.com REST endpoint historically DOES, but the multipart :generateContent shape with inline images is less battle-tested in the browser.

If either blocks browser-origin calls, the entire BYOK architecture collapses. Salvageable Phase 1 work is zero — must pivot before Phase 1 starts.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- scripts/cors-smoke.html (standalone, opened directly in a browser; not a Next.js page yet)
- For each provider, three calls + Network tab observation:
  1. POST /v1/images/generations (OpenAI) with minimal body
  2. GET /v1/models?key=... (Gemini)
  3. POST models/{id}:generateContent (Gemini)
- Document outcome in docs/plans/vucible.md §14.T (T1/T2/T3 branch).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- For each call: preflight OPTIONS succeeds OR is unnecessary; actual request returns response body (not opaque). PASS if all three succeed.
- If FAIL: document the blocked endpoint + status, present pivot options to author, get explicit decision.

DECISION GATE:
- T1 (both pass) → proceed unchanged.
- T2 (OpenAI blocks) → author favors thin Vercel Edge proxy for OpenAI only (new DD-024 documenting the deviation from DD-001). Phase 1+ adapts to proxy-shaped paths.
- T3 (Gemini blocks) → drop Gemini in v1; wizard tier dropdown already supports OpenAI-only flow.

TEST SCOPE: manual browser test, real keys. Author absorbs ~$0.04 OpenAI cost for the test-gen call.

RISKS:
- Author's chosen origin (file:// vs localhost vs production-like) may produce different CORS results than the actual deploy origin (vercel.app or custom). Run the test from at least localhost AND a vercel-app preview URL before declaring T1.
- A 'works in some browsers' pattern (Chrome lax, Firefox strict) is functionally a fail.

DOWNSTREAM: Phase 1+ ALL phases depend on this resolving favorably. Critical path bead — PRIORITY 0.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §9.6, §14.T, DD-001.
close_reason: T1 confirmed: both providers CORS-clean from browser. OpenAI preflights pass (Allow-Origin: *); Gemini fully CORS-clean. One caveat: OpenAI 401s on image endpoints lack CORS headers (Cloudflare); workaround is validate key via /v1/models first. No proxy needed. 7 assertions in cors-smoke.test.ts all pass.

title: Manual smoke-test script for OpenAI + Gemini (resolves §14.A / §14.B)
description:

title: README rewrite + user-facing docs (BYOK warning, run, deploy, key rotation)
description:
WHY: Current README.md is the create-next-app boilerplate ('npm run dev' etc.). For a single-user BYOK app the operator (the author + future agents resuming) needs a real README with: what vucible is, BYOK security model, how to run locally with bun (not npm), how to deploy, and how to rotate / revoke keys. Without this, a session-resuming agent has to re-read the plan files to learn what's going on, and a fresh-clone deploy is undocumented.

This is also the AGENTS.md template's escape hatch — the file currently has '<!-- CUSTOMIZE -->' placeholders for Project Architecture and Repo Layout that should be filled in once code stabilizes (pulled in this bead because both surfaces target the same audience).

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- README.md (replace boilerplate):
  - Header: 'vucible — Visualization Crucible. Browser-only image-prompt evolution tool. BYOK (no backend).'
  - 'What it does' (3-bullet summary mirroring §1).
  - 'BYOK security model' (200 words): keys live in browser localStorage; never proxied; copy-paste at your own risk; clear via Settings → Clear all keys.
  - 'Run locally': bun install + bun run dev. Explicit 'do NOT use npm/yarn/pnpm per AGENTS.md'.
  - 'Run tests': bun run test (unit) + e2e instructions once Phase 11 lands.
  - 'Deploy': link to Phase 12 deploy bead; 'output: export' rationale (DD-020).
  - 'Key rotation runbook' (operator-facing):
    1. To rotate an OpenAI key: revoke at platform.openai.com → in vucible Settings → KeysPanel → Clear → re-paste new key → click Validate.
    2. Same for Gemini at aistudio.google.com.
    3. localStorage scope = origin; rotation invalidates other tabs (storage event listener notifies them; they show 'key cleared' state).
  - 'Where the data lives' (1 paragraph): localStorage['vucible:v1'] for keys + defaults; IndexedDB ('vucible-history') for rounds + image bytes; both wiped via Settings → Clear all.
  - 'Plan files' link: docs/plans/vucible.md (master) + docs/plans/wizard.md (FR-8 detail).

- AGENTS.md updates (additive — do NOT delete the existing scaffolding sections):
  - Replace the '<!-- CUSTOMIZE -->' Project Architecture section with the actual 7-module breakdown from vucible.md §2.3 (Wizard | App Shell | Round Engine | Storage | Providers | UI | Tests).
  - Replace the '<!-- CUSTOMIZE -->' Repo Layout section with the actual src/ tree from vucible.md §2.4.
  - Add a 'Vucible-specific conventions' section (~10 lines): never log api keys (point at lib/log.ts trauma-guard), aspect-ratio invariant (§5.1 + §10.7 triple-coverage), MIME preservation on multipart parts (§10.4), errors pass through NormalizedError + errorToMessage helper (vucible-q8e).
  - DO NOT TOUCH the RULE 1 (no deletions), Node toolchain, or Landing the Plane sections — those are user-set invariants.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- README.md no longer contains 'create-next-app', 'npm run dev', or 'yarn dev'.
- bun run dev / bun run build / bun run test all documented.
- Key-rotation steps are accurate (verifiable by following them on a deployed instance).
- AGENTS.md retains all original sections; only the placeholder areas + new conventions section change.
- A fresh-clone agent can read README + AGENTS.md and run the app + understand the architecture without reading docs/plans/.

TEST SCOPE: manual review.

ESTIMATE: ~45 min. Lands late in Phase 12 (after deploy is real and rotation paths are tested).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §1, §2.3, §2.4, §5, §13, DD-001, DD-020. Surfaced by polish round 4 (lens 8 — documentation deliverables).

title: Install runtime dependencies (ulid, idb, msw, fake-indexeddb)
description:
Install via bun (per AGENTS.md never npm/yarn/pnpm).

DEPS:
- ulid (~1KB, no deps) — for ULID generation in src/lib/storage/schema.ts. monotonicFactory() required to prevent same-ms duplicate IDs (round orchestration generates round + slot IDs in tight bursts within a single ms).
- idb (Jake Archibald) — tiny promise wrapper around IndexedDB. Uses global indexedDB so the polyfill (fake-indexeddb) must be imported BEFORE any idb import in test files.
- msw v2 — for unit tests of provider clients. v2 uses native fetch/Request/Response interceptors; node-mode setup goes in vitest.setup.ts via setupServer(...). v2 + jsdom 24 may have issues — pin compatible versions.
- fake-indexeddb — REQUIRED: jsdom does not implement IndexedDB. Import 'fake-indexeddb/auto' at the top of vitest.setup.ts; without this every storage/history test errors with 'indexedDB is not defined'. Non-negotiable.

VERSION COMPAT VERIFICATION: check React 19 / Next 16 / Node 22 compatibility for each. msw v2 is the most likely to surface friction; pin to a version that's confirmed-working with jsdom + bun-or-node test runner per §14.AB.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- 'bun install' completes cleanly.
- 'bun run build' still passes after install.
- A trivial test importing ulid + idb + fake-indexeddb/auto + msw setupServer runs successfully.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §3, Phase 0 sub-task 3, §14.AB.
close_reason: All 4 deps installed and verified: ulid@3.0.2, idb@8.0.3, msw@2.14.2, fake-indexeddb@6.2.5. Build passes.

title: Orphan-round sweep on AppShell mount
description:
WHY: Without this sweep, orphan rounds linger forever with 'loading' slots that the UI would render as in-progress shimmers in the history rail. Resolves §10.4 'Startup orphan-round sweep' requirement.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- A mount-time effect inside <AppShell/> that calls history.findOrphanRounds() and for each: rewrites in a single transaction — every slot still in 'loading' state becomes {status:'error', kind:'network_error', message:'Round interrupted (refresh or tab close).'}; settledAt = now().
- Idempotent: if no orphans, no-op. Failure of the sweep (IDB error) is logged with console.warn and tolerated — the app proceeds anyway. Per-slot fallback in <RoundCard/> treats unknown-status slots as 'error' so unhandled orphans render gracefully.

DEPENDS ON: Phase 1 history.ts (findOrphanRounds + markRoundOrphaned helpers).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Test: Pre-populate IDB with a Round having settledAt:null and slots in 'loading' state. Mount <AppShell/>. After mount, the round in IDB has settledAt set and all loading slots flipped to error.
- Sweep failure (mock IDB throw) does not crash the app.

TEST SCOPE: integration (component + fake-indexeddb).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §10.4 'Startup orphan-round sweep', §11 Phase 4 sub-task 5, §14.AC.
notes: Polish round 4: orphan-sweep already documents console.warn on IDB error; replace with vucible-qsv.2 logEvent({event:'history.orphan.swept', count, level: count>0 ? 'warn' : 'debug'}). Use IDB-snapshot fixture vucible-qsv.1 orphanRoundDb() in tests.
close_reason: Orphan-round sweep wired into AppShell useEffect. Calls findOrphanRounds() then markRoundOrphaned() for each orphan. IDB errors silently absorbed (no app crash). Tests mock storage/history module.

title: Wizard scratchpad: wizard-progress.ts
description:
WHY: Mid-wizard refresh safety. Separate from VucibleStorageV1 so a partial wizard never appears as a 'configured' state. Wiped on completion.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/storage/wizard-progress.ts:
  interface WizardProgress {
    step: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4;
    draftProviders: Partial<Record<Provider, { apiKey?; tier?; ipm?; validatedAt?; error?: NormalizedError }>>;
    draftDefaults?: Partial<UserDefaults>;
  }
  - getProgress(): WizardProgress | null
  - setProgress(p): void — debounced ~250ms internally OR caller-debounced; pick one and document.
  - clearProgress(): void — called on wizard 'complete' action.

NOTE: Don't write on every keystroke — only on validation outcomes and step changes. Validation results + step transitions are the only persistence-worthy events (per wizard.md §6.4).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Round-trip across all WizardProgress states (step 1–4, with/without draftProviders, with/without draftDefaults).
- clearProgress() removes the key cleanly.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §5.2, docs/plans/wizard.md §6.2, §6.4.

title: Full keyboard-only walkthrough + ARIA roles/labels
description:

WHAT IT PRODUCES:

  - Focus rings visible on shadcn primitives (verify base-ui-backed shadcn 4.5 doesn't drop focus rings).
  - Aspect picker buttons: arrow-key navigation in DiscreteRatioGrid.
  - Image cards: focusable; Space/Enter to select; Shift+click to multi-select; Esc to clear.
  - Error tiles: role='status' aria-live='polite'.
  - Toasts: aria-live='assertive'.
  - Color contrast all text ≥ 4.5:1.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §9.1, §11 Phase 11 sub-task 1, §16 acceptance #8.

title: Stable per-slot keys for streaming render (composite roundId:provider:index)
description:
WHY: Per §11 Phase 6 sub-task 8. Slots aren't IDed in §5.3 schema (do not add slotId field — derive at render time). title: Schema migration registry scaffold (§14.Y)
description:
WHY: Without this, any future schemaVersion bump silently wipes returning users. SEMI-URGENT round-5 finding (§14.Y).

§14.D leans toward 'treat malformed → wizard runs with banner'. As written, that path also fires for VALID BUT OLDER schema versions — so a v2 upgrade that bumps schemaVersion would silently force every returning user back through the wizard and lose their history (IndexedDB stays, but the keys/defaults blob is wiped).

Plan must distinguish:
- schemaVersion missing or unparseable JSON → treat as fresh install, run wizard.
- schemaVersion < CURRENT_VERSION → run a registered migration from src/lib/storage/migrations/v{N}-to-v{N+1}.ts.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/storage/migrations/index.ts: empty Map<number, MigrationFn> registry + applyMigrations(stored, currentVersion) function.
- src/lib/storage/migrations/README.md or header comment documenting the contract: each migration takes a deserialized object at version N and returns one at N+1. Pure function. No side effects on localStorage; caller persists.
- src/lib/storage/keys.ts integration: getStorage() detects schemaVersion mismatch and runs applyMigrations before returning.

v1 has NO migrations to write, but the registry + version-gate logic must exist from day one. Otherwise v2 is a breaking release for every user.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Empty registry compiles.
- getStorage() with schemaVersion=1 returns the blob unchanged (no migrations applied).
- getStorage() with schemaVersion=0 (synthesized in test) calls a stub migration and returns the migrated shape.
- getStorage() with schemaVersion=undefined / malformed JSON returns null (handled as fresh install, wizard runs).

TEST SCOPE: unit. Synthesize fake schemaVersion=0 blob, register a stub v0→v1 migration, verify migration ran exactly once.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §14.Y (locked), §14.D, §11 Phase 1.
close_reason: Migration registry + applyMigrations shipped. 8 tests pass: version match, gap, chain, null cases, immutability.

title: withRetry helper + MAX_RETRY_AFTER_MS cap
description:
WHY: Per §6.5 / DD-019. 3x retry budget with exponential backoff for retryable errors (rate_limited, server_error, network_error). Honors Retry-After header up to MAX_RETRY_AFTER_MS=60_000 cap.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/round/retry.ts:
  withRetry<T>(fn: (signal: AbortSignal) => Promise<T>, opts: { signal: AbortSignal; maxAttempts?: 3 }): Promise<T>
  - On retryable error: wait Retry-After (clamped to MAX_RETRY_AFTER_MS) if server provided one, otherwise jittered exp-backoff (1s, 2s, 4s + ≤250ms jitter).
  - If retryAfter requested > MAX_RETRY_AFTER_MS: throw immediately (don't hold throttle slot for an hour). User can manually click Regenerate.
  - On non-retryable error or attempt cap reached: throw.
- Module-level constant: MAX_RETRY_AFTER_MS = 60_000.

THROTTLE INTERACTION: Orchestrator wraps each provider call in throttle.enqueue(() => withRetry(fn)). Retries happen INSIDE the throttled function — slot held for entire retry chain. Worst case ~45s slot hold (per §6.5 rationale). v2 candidate per §14.R.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- 3 attempts on a retryable error, then throws.
- Non-retryable error throws on attempt 1.
- Retry-After: 5 → waits 5s before retry.
- Retry-After: 3600 (over cap) → throws immediately.
- AbortSignal: aborting mid-wait propagates as AbortError.

TEST SCOPE: unit with vitest fake timers; verify wait durations precisely (await vi.runAllTimersAsync() between attempts).

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §6.5, §11 Phase 6 sub-task 2, DD-019, §14.R.
close_reason: withRetry + 11 tests shipped

title: IndexedDB history.ts (sessions + rounds CRUD + idb wrapper)
description:
WHY: Round + session persistence layer. Used by orchestrator (Phase 6) for eager-intent placeholder + batched settle write, and by AppShell mount (Phase 4) for orphan-round sweep.

WHAT IT PRODUCES:
- src/lib/storage/history.ts (uses 'idb' npm package):
  - openHistoryDB(): Promise<IDBPDatabase> — opens vucible-history v1; creates sessions + rounds object stores with indexes (sessions.by_startedAt; rounds.by_sessionId, rounds.by_startedAt).
  - createSession(prompt: string): Promise<Session>
  - appendRoundToSession(sessionId, roundId): Promise<void>
  - putRoundPlaceholder(round: Round): Promise<void> — eager-intent write (§10.4 step 6)
  - finalizeRound(round: Round): Promise<void> — batched settle write; single transaction overwrites placeholder
  - getRound(id): Promise<Round | undefined>
  - listRoundsBySession(sessionId): Promise<Round[]>
  - listSessions(limit): Promise<Session[]>
  - findOrphanRounds(): Promise<Round[]> — for §10.4 orphan-round sweep on AppShell mount.
  - markRoundOrphaned(round, reason): Promise<void> — sweep helper that flips loading slots to error+settledAt=now, single transaction.

CRITICAL §14.V: ArrayBuffer round-trip. idb's put() calls structuredClone under the hood. ArrayBuffer is cloneable (not transferred) by default unless we explicitly opt into a transfer. Verify: write a Round, mutate the in-memory ArrayBuffer post-write, read back from IDB — bytes should be unchanged. If transfers do occur (semantics get weird inside an Object containing an ArrayBuffer), wrap bytes in a Blob before IDB write. Cheap to verify, expensive to discover late.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All CRUD round-trips succeed under fake-indexeddb.
- ArrayBuffer round-trip preserves bytes (post-write mutation does not corrupt persisted blob).
- findOrphanRounds returns rounds with settledAt === null.
- Single-transaction settle: putRoundPlaceholder followed by finalizeRound results in one final committed Round (placeholder overwritten).

TEST SCOPE: unit (with fake-indexeddb). Specifically include §14.V ArrayBuffer round-trip verification test.

Refs: docs/plans/vucible.md §5.3, §10.4, §14.V.
close_reason: history.ts: full CRUD (sessions+rounds), singleton DB, orphan detection+sweep, placeholder→finalize. 12 tests pass incl §14.V ArrayBuffer round-trip. 78 total tests green.
